name: feat-ESE-21
title: Tilemap system with carving and interactive demo
description:
Build the tilemap system described in docs/tilemap-architecture.md, with an interactive demo.

### 1. TileSet system
- TileSet, TileSetSource, TileData with arbitrary polygon tiles (vertices in [0,1] local space)
- TileMapLayer with sparse HashMap storage
- Chunk-based organisation (32x32 tiles per chunk)
- Coordinate system: lower-left corner of lower-left cell at (0,0), centre at (0.5, 0.5)

### 2. Terrain carving
- DensityGrid per chunk (2 subcells per tile side by default)
- Carving subtracts polygons from tiles by zeroing density values
- Marching squares extracts terrain boundary for collision and rendering
- First carve initialises density grid from tile polygon (arbitrary shapes supported)

### 3. Interactive demo
- Scene using existing camera/renderquad/node structure
- Variety of tile types displayed (full, triangle, half, custom shapes)
- Tile painting: select a tile type, click to place
- Tile carving: select a carving polygon, move mouse, click to carve
- Reset: restore tilemap to full or empty
- Debug overlays: grid lines, subdivided grid, collision boundaries, chunk boundaries, density values

### 4. Integration
- Chunks as ECS entities with DensityGrid, PhysicsCollider, RenderBatch, DirtyFlag components
- Systems iterate only dirty chunks
- Uses existing renderer for visualisation
acceptanceCriteria:
- TileSet with multiple tile types (full, triangle, half, custom polygon)
- TileMapLayer places tiles at grid positions
- Carving subtracts arbitrary polygons from tiles via density grid
- Marching squares produces correct terrain boundaries after carving
- Interactive demo: paint tiles, carve shapes, reset
- Debug overlays toggle: grid, subdivided grid, collision edges, chunk bounds
- Coordinate system correct: (0,0) at lower-left corner, (0.5,0.5) at centre
- Uses existing camera/scene/node/renderer infrastructure
demoDeliverable: Interactive browser demo showing: (1) a tilemap with variety of tile shapes, (2) painting tiles by clicking, (3) carving tiles with a polygon tool, (4) debug overlay showing grid, density, collision edges, and chunk boundaries.
testingScenarios:
- Create tileset with 4+ tile types, verify polygons render correctly
- Place tiles at various positions, verify coordinate system (0,0 at lower-left)
- Carve a circle from a full tile, verify density grid updates and marching squares produces correct boundary
- Carve across chunk boundaries, verify both chunks update
- Toggle debug overlays, verify grid/collision/chunk visualisation
- Reset tilemap to full, verify all tiles restored
- Reset to empty, verify all tiles cleared
stakeholderUnderstanding: Creating a tileset system with vertex-accurate points for raw tiles, subdividing to smaller grids for polygon subtraction on carved tiles. Demo showcases painting, carving, reset, and debug overlays using existing camera/renderquad/scene/node infrastructure.
